---
title: "City2Graph: Easy graph generation in Python"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-04-18 18:33"
image: "city2graph.png"
description: "#City2Graph is an open-source #Python package that turns geospatial data into #graph structures for #NetworkAnalysis and Graph Neural Networks, with seamless integration into tools like #GeoPandas, #NetworkX, and #PyTorch Geometric. It supports diverse data sources from #OSM and #GTFS to #OMF making relevant spatial datasets easy to access and work with."
---

[City2Graph][website] is a new^[Currently v. 0.3.1.] Python package for 
transforming geospatial data into graph representations for Graph Neural
Networks[^gnns] and for network analysis. It's [open-source][repo] under the 
BSD-3-Clause license. 

It integrates well with [`GeoPandas`][geopandas][^geopandas], 
[`NetworkX`][networkx][^networkx], and [`Pytorch Geometric`][pytorch-geometric][^pytorch-geometric], works with data of different domains (for example, 
streets, public transit, origin-destionation matrices, POIs, and more) and can 
use various data sources such as OSM[^osm], OMF[^omf], and publishers of 
GTFS[^gtfs] data. [City2Graph][website] makes it particularly easy to work with 
(initially not very easy to obtain) OMF data.

![City2Graph inputs and outputs (source: [Yuta Sato][article-1])](overview.webp "City2Graph inputs and outputs (source: Yuta Sato)")

Yuta Sato, City2Graph's lead developer, has published two interesting articles 
on the package: 

- [City2Graph: Python package for spatial network analysis and GeoAI with GNNs][article-1] introduces City2Graph, its main features, and how it fits into the 
system of Python-based spatial data analysis packages.

- In [How to Use Overture Maps Like OSMnx – by City2Graph][article-2], Yuto 
covers how City2Graph can be used to work with, and transform, OMF data.

[repo]: https://github.com/c2g-dev/city2graph
[website]: https://city2graph.net/
[geopandas]: https://geopandas.org/
[networkx]: https://networkx.org/
[pytorch-geometric]: https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io
[article-1]: https://medium.com/@yuta.sato.now/city2graph-a-python-package-for-spatial-network-analysis-and-graph-neural-networks-gnns-bc943dd6d85e
[article-2]: https://medium.com/@yuta.sato.now/how-to-use-overture-maps-like-osmnx-by-city2graph-7e01d38f9f61

[^gnns]: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are a class of deep learning models designed to operate directly on graph-structured data by learning representations through aggregating information from neighboring nodes and edges.
[^geopandas]: `GeoPandas` is an extension to the popular data science library `Pandas` that enables support for geospatial data.
[^networkx]: `NetworkX` is a Python library for the creation, manipulation, and study of networks and graphs.
[^pytorch-geometric]: `PyTorch Geometric` is a library built on `PyTorch` for deep learning on graph-structured data.
[^osm]: [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/), a collaborative, open-licensed map of the world.
[^omf]: The [Overture Maps Foundation](https://overturemaps.org/) is an industry consortium (members include Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and TomTom) that produces openly-licensed map data, building primarily on OpenStreetMap data.
[^gtfs]: The General Transit Feed Specification is a data standard defining a common format for public transit schedules and associated geographic information.
